intro: The clearest dividing line between Azure Key Vault and Cycode is access: Azure Key Vault offers a free tier you can start on, while Cycode requires a paid plan from day one (subscription from $1). If you need to prototype before committing budget, that matters. Beyond the headline, Azure Key Vault carries low lock-in (azure-bound secrets) and subscription from $1, with a free tier; Cycode carries medium lock-in (supply chain saas, replaceable) and subscription from $1. Azure Key Vault is a fit when you are teams. Cycode is a fit when you are teams. The honest trade-off: Azure Key Vault is explicitly not for solo, static projects; Cycode is explicitly not for solo, static projects. On the product side, Azure Key Vault is described as Managed HSM and secrets service storing keys, certificates, and connection strings with Azure AD access control, and Cycode is described as Software supply chain security platform scanning secrets, IaC, SCA, and SAST across the entire SDLC.
quick_take: Azure Key Vault is for teams; Cycode is for teams; decide on whether you need a free tier.
verdict_a: Choose Azure Key Vault if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches teams.
verdict_b: Choose Cycode if its medium lock-in is acceptable for your use case and you are teams.

faq.q: Is Azure Key Vault cheaper than Cycode?
faq.a: Azure Key Vault starts at $1; Cycode starts at $1. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Azure Key Vault to Cycode?
faq.a: Migration from Azure Key Vault to Cycode is plausible because Azure Key Vault has low lock-in (azure-bound secrets). You would still have to map config, permissions and integrations to Cycode's model. Plan for dual-run and rollback.

On our scoring Azure Key Vault rates 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Cycode. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Azure Key Vault. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Cycode a good alternative to Azure Key Vault?
faq.a: Cycode targets the same best-fit audience as Azure Key Vault (teams). Note that Cycode is explicitly not for solo, static projects, so it only replaces Azure Key Vault if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (low vs medium), which is the main thing to weigh.
